title: Throat clearing
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports cough duration and character, sputum, smoking, medicines, occupation and environment, wheeze, rhinitis, heartburn, functional and mood impact, and face-to-face upper-airway, ear and chest examination.
assessment.rationale: The exact adult chronic-cough section supports duration, throat-clearing or cough context, triggers, smoking, medicine, rhinitis, reflux, functional impact and upper-airway examination without assigning a cause.

source.title: British Thoracic Society Clinical Statement on chronic cough in adults
source.population: Adults with chronic cough lasting more than 8 weeks.
source.setting: Primary and specialist assessment of chronic cough.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult chronic cough assessment and referral; not applicable to children and not directly validated for UAE pathways.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports cough duration and character, sputum, smoking, medicines, occupation and environment, wheeze, rhinitis, heartburn, functional and mood impact, and face-to-face upper-airway, ear and chest examination.

assessment.text: Supports chest radiography, spirometry with reversibility, sputum testing where infection is suspected, selected inflammatory markers, specialist referral for persistent or unclear cough, and upper-airway specialist assessment when indicated.
assessment.rationale: The exact investigation context supports recording reviewed specialist information and a clinician-entered plan or follow-up without ordering tests or treatment.

source.title: British Thoracic Society Clinical Statement on chronic cough in adults
source.population: Adults with chronic cough lasting more than 8 weeks.
source.setting: Primary and specialist assessment of chronic cough.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult chronic cough assessment and referral; not applicable to children and not directly validated for UAE pathways.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports chest radiography, spirometry with reversibility, sputum testing where infection is suspected, selected inflammatory markers, specialist referral for persistent or unclear cough, and upper-airway specialist assessment when indicated.

sources_used.title: British Thoracic Society Clinical Statement on chronic cough in adults
